name: CellTypist (Automated Cell Type Annotation for scRNA-seq)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Teichmann Lab / Wellcome Sanger Institute

auth.notes: Local library; model downloads from CellTypist server

performance.notes: LogisticRegression backend; fast inference; initial model download needed

agent_readiness.notes: Automated cell type annotation for scRNA-seq. import celltypist. celltypist.models.download_models(force_update=True). model = celltypist.models.Model.load(model='Immune_All_Low.pkl'). predictions = celltypist.annotate(adata, model='Immune_All_Low.pkl', majority_voting=True). adata.obs['majority_voting'] = predictions.predicted_labels.majority_voting. adata.obs['conf_score'] = predictions.probability_matrix.max(axis=1). Custom training: celltypist.train(adata, labels='cell_type', n_jobs=10). 50+ pre-trained immune and tissue models.

gotchas: Requires normalized and log1p-transformed counts (not raw) as input
Model specificity varies — immune models work well, tissue-specific may not
majority_voting requires a Scanpy neighborhood graph (spatial grouping)
Custom training requires thousands of labeled cells per type
best_when: Automated immune cell type annotation (PBMC, bone marrow, blood)
Quick cell annotation without manual marker gene curation
Hierarchical cell type prediction with majority voting
Training custom models on labeled scRNA-seq data
avoid_when: Non-immune tissue types without available CellTypist model
When manual annotation with marker genes is preferred
Rare cell types with low abundance
summary: CellTypist provides automated cell type annotation for scRNA-seq. celltypist.annotate(adata, model='Immune_All_Low.pkl', majority_voting=True). predictions.predicted_labels. probability_matrix. 50+ pretrained models (immune, tissue). Custom training. MIT by Teichmann Lab/Sanger. Best for immune cell annotation in PBMC/blood datasets. Requires log1p-normalized input. majority_voting needs Scanpy neighbor graph.
